div1.entryFree:
Bald, 1) destitute of hair or of natural covering in general: Tp. IV, 238. Err. II, 2, 71. Err. II, 2, 71 Err. II, 2, 71 Err. II, 2, 71 As IV, 3, 106. John III, 1, 324. H4A II, 4, 420. H4B III, 2, 294. H5 V, 2, 169. Cor. II, 3, 21. III, 1, 164.* Tim. IV, 3, 160. Lr. I, 4, 178. No question asked him by any of the senators, but they stand b. before him, Cor. IV, 5, 206, i. e. uncovering their heads, they stand in their natural baldness before him.
2) void of reason, unfounded: 'twould be a b. conclusion, Err. II, 2, 110. this b. unjointed chat, H4A I, 3, 65.
